name: gargoyle
description: A graph memory database MCP server for agent memories and seeding context
long_description:
Gargoyle is a typed knowledge graph MCP server that provides persistent memory for AI agents. It stores entities (people, projects, decisions, notes, ideas, tasks, commitments) with structured canonical fields, rich markdown bodies, and graph relations between them.

**KEY FEATURES**
- **Typed Entity System**: Entities with canonical field schemas per type (note, idea, task, person, project, commitment, etc.)
- **Full-Text Search**: FTS5-powered keyword search across all entity titles and bodies
- **Semantic Search**: Embedding-based similarity search for finding conceptually related entities
- **Graph Traversal**: BFS traversal to explore entity neighborhoods and discover connections
- **Claims & Provenance**: Subject-predicate-object triples with evidence linking and run-based provenance
- **Auto-Memory Hooks**: Python hooks that automatically search on every prompt and extract observations after every response
- **Deduplication**: Built-in duplicate detection and merge suggestions

**REQUIREMENTS**
- **Gargoyle MCP binary** (Rust) — the core MCP server
- **Python 3.10+** — for the auto-memory hook scripts
- **httpx** — `pip install httpx`

db_path.title: Database Path
db_path.description: Path to the gargoyle SQLite database file

erasmus_text_url.title: Erasmus Text Inference URL
erasmus_text_url.description: OpenAI-compatible chat completions endpoint for keyword extraction and observation extraction

erasmus_embed_url.title: Erasmus Embedding URL
erasmus_embed_url.description: Embedding endpoint for semantic similarity search

tools.name: create_entity
tools.description: Create a typed entity in the knowledge graph with canonical fields (e.g. person, project, note, task, idea, commitment)

tools.name: get_entity
tools.description: Get an entity by its UUID

tools.name: update_entity
tools.description: Update an entity's fields with optimistic locking via expected_updated_at

tools.name: delete_entity
tools.description: Soft-delete an entity by UUID

tools.name: list_entities
tools.description: List all entities, optionally filtered by entity type

tools.name: search_fts
tools.description: Full-text search across all entities (title, body, canonical fields)

tools.name: search_similar
tools.description: Semantic similarity search using embeddings

tools.name: get_entity_graph
tools.description: BFS traversal of the entity graph from a starting node

tools.name: create_relation
tools.description: Create a typed relation (edge) between two entities

tools.name: get_relations
tools.description: Get all relations for an entity (both incoming and outgoing)

tools.name: list_claims
tools.description: List claims (subject-predicate-object triples), optionally filtered by evidence entity

tools.name: get_claim
tools.description: Get a specific claim by UUID

tools.name: promote_claim
tools.description: Promote a claim to a full entity in the knowledge graph

tools.name: apply_patch_set
tools.description: Apply a batch of atomic patch operations (create/update entities, relations, claims)

tools.name: check_duplicates
tools.description: Check an entity for potential duplicates in the knowledge graph

tools.name: list_dedup_suggestions
tools.description: List dedup suggestions, optionally filtered by status

tools.name: audit_relations
tools.description: Audit all relation types for consistency and report issues

tools.name: get_template
tools.description: Get a template definition by its key

tools.name: list_templates
tools.description: List available templates, optionally filtered by category

tools.name: get_context
tools.description: Get operational context value by key

tools.name: set_context
tools.description: Set operational context (key-value store for workflow state)
